operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $51.9 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 14%, gasoline price increases contributing 390 basis points, and 25 net new warehouses opened since prior year. E-commerce comparable sales grew 13% while foreign currency headwinds reduced net sales by 64 basis points. Membership fee revenue increased 10% to $967 million with renewal rates at 92% U.S./Canada and 90% worldwide, benefiting from auto-renewals and Executive membership upgrades.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 32 basis points to 10.64% primarily from core merchandise category declines and LIFO charges, partially offset by ancillary business improvements. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales improved 94 basis points to 8.98% due to sales leverage and cessation of prior year COVID-19 incremental wages. Operating income increased 35% to $1.8 billion with operating margin expanding to 3.5%.
material_events: Board appointed Ron Vachris as President and COO effective February 2022 with Craig Jelinek continuing as CEO. Company repaid $800 million Senior Notes prior to maturity and declared quarterly dividend of $0.79 per share. Share repurchases totaled $83 million under $4 billion authorization with $3.1 billion remaining capacity. Management announced mid-March wage and benefit increases with estimated $275 million annualized incremental costs plus $85 million third quarter true-up.
macro_exposures: Merchandise costs impacted by inflation higher than recent years with supply and logistics constraints continuing across certain categories. Foreign exchange rate changes negatively impacted net sales from international operations. Tariff uncertainty persists across China, United States and United Kingdom affecting merchandise costs with exposure dependent on goods type, rates and timing.
forward_outlook: Management expects COVID-related supply constraints to continue with wage increases adding approximately $275 million in annualized pre-tax costs. Company plans capital expenditures of approximately $4.0 billion for fiscal 2022 focused on new warehouse development and infrastructure. Management expects to open 15-18 additional warehouses in remainder of fiscal 2022 with higher growth rates expected internationally.
uncertainty_profile: Company faces labor-related legal proceedings under California Labor Code including seating, wage payment and overtime claims across various jurisdictions. Opioid litigation consolidated in federal proceedings includes claims from counties, cities and third-party payors. Capital expenditure plans and new warehouse opening timeline carry execution risk given construction, permitting variables and evolving economic environment.
